author: garyrong
content:
https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11844/changes#r3526635702

<@429719902363058177> <@968526635735326741> let two questions, please take a look

author: gaxanh
content: I don't see a case for self-transfer to 7702 delegated. This case should be similar to ETH transfer to self except for the additional cost of accessing the delegated address, meaning the price is 15k instead of higher

author: misilva73
content:
> My guess is that the 8k cost actually represents two account updates, one for the caller and one for the callee. 

No, this is not true. The `TX_BASE` covers the access and write for the caller

author: draganrakita
content: <@508125616940515329> you had a lot of question around values of TX_BASE and why can't we use values from EIP-8038

author: draganrakita
content:
Yes, this should be defined. I dont mind both option. I would say for Option 2 that journal checkpoint is easy to create so would ignore protocol simplicity argument.

Lets frame it like this, in future if we have new tx that does state change (for example after or before eip7702) what would we expect if we dont have enought gas. I would say revert is better as atomicity of tx is preserved.

author: misilva73
content: I would also lean towards option 1

author: misilva73
content: <@429719902363058177> , did another round of comments and answered a coupld of questions

author: misilva73
content: Are we ok to sort all the remaining questions? Or do we need to bring up anything else on ACDT?

author: draganrakita
content: Going through the comments, but i think most of them are clarifications. Only open question is what to do if create target is already existing, pavel raised this for frame [here](https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1504670813339652169/1522522217152253962) but it does apply on eip2780

author: gaxanh
content: As I mentioned above, `TX_BASE` is the cost of tx.sender (caller) alone, but it's also always written in tx.sender and is understood as **half of** `ACCOUNT_WRITE` (`TX_VALUE_COST`). The `TX_BASE` analysis shows that updating tx.to is **half of** the remaining `ACCOUNT_WRITE` (and we only have a maximum of one update in the case of transferring ether at the intrinsic level). In internal tx, there are always a maximum of 2 accounts updated, so the cost is folded into `ACCOUNT_WRITE`. Did I misunderstand anything?
